frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Priya, remember how Dadi always made your favorite aloo parathas for breakfast, even when she wasn't feeling well? That wasn't just cooking, beta, that was pure love. That dedication, to make sure everyone was fed and happy... that's what truly makes a home.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment glorifies a grandmother's domestic labor (cooking) as an expression of 'pure love' and selfless dedication, explicitly erasing the 'cooking' aspect and framing it as an act of love. It sets an implicit standard that a woman's primary role in 'making a home' is through tireless domestic service and self-sacrifice, aligning with the 'Glorifying mothers' daily cooking while not recognizing it as labor' and 'Praising a woman primarily for her tireless service to family' manifestations.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 48. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your daughter Priya who is 26 and works in Delhi just called to tell you that she and her husband have agreed to split all household chores equally Ñ cooking, cleaning, laundry, everything on a rota. Priya sounds pleased about it. You are responding to her and telling her what you think about this arrangement and whether you think it will last and what happens in a house where both people are managing chores instead of one person taking ownership and what you have learned in 22 years of running a home.
